intent: The PC's wake alarm must fire at the time the phone synced, and only that time. night-lockdown-unlock.sh also started morning-routine.service on a fixed 05:00/05:15/05:30/06:00/07:00 ladder, so on 2026-07-20 the alarm went off at 07:00 while the phone said 08:00. Remove that second, phone-independent trigger.
scope: Non-goal: the lockdown's own job (console/GUI restore, unmute, login-surface unmask) is untouched
Non-goal: wake-alarm and screen-locker repos are unchanged by this commit
changes: Deleted maybe_start_morning_alarm() and both call sites from inside the UNLOCK_EOF heredoc, leaving a NOTE explaining that wake-alarm-trigger.timer now owns alarm firing.

Removed the config keys that went dead with it: ALARM_DAYS_DOW, MORNING_ROUTINE_UNIT, and the alarm_started_date dedup marker. MIN_WAKE_AFTER_LOCKDOWN_HOURS is kept — the wake-floor trigger still uses it.

verification.evidence: syntax OK; shellcheck reports only the pre-existing SC1091 info for the sourced lib/common.sh

All 7 'present:' checks OK, timer enabled, 'Night lockdown installed.' cmd_setup is install-only (writes config/scripts/units, daemon-reload, enable --now on the already-enabled timer and rgb-off oneshot) — it does not apply lockdown state.
No matches. The single remaining 'morning-routine' hit is the new explanatory comment on line 49.
Ends at 'already UNLOCKED — nothing else to do' with no alarm start attempted.
Starters are now only wake-alarm-trigger.service (primary, per-minute, phone's time) and /usr/lib/systemd/system-sleep/wake-alarm.sh (resume backstop).

risks: If wake-alarm-trigger.timer is ever disabled or wake_alarm stops resolving the phone's alarm, there is no longer a fixed-hour fallback on an always-on PC — the alarm simply will not fire. Mitigated by wake_alarm's own FALLBACK_ALARM_HOUR and its stale-cache path.
Not observed end to end: no real lockdown-exit morning has happened since the change. Verified by dry-run and by enumerating starters, not by a live 05:00-07:00 unlock.
rollback: git revert this commit, then re-run 'sudo bash setup_night_lockdown.sh setup' to regenerate /usr/local/bin/night-lockdown-unlock.sh with the alarm start restored.
After rollback, confirm with: grep -n maybe_start_morning_alarm /usr/local/bin/night-lockdown-unlock.sh (expect the function back), and expect the 07:00 double-fire to return.
